title: Entropy of manifolds and of their fundamental group
speaker: Gerard Besson
abstract: Abstract : It is a joint work with G. Courtois, S. Gallot and A.Sambusetti. We prove a compactness theorem for metric spaces with anupper bound on the entropy and other conditions that will be discussed.Several finiteness results will be drawn. It is a work in progress.
tags: Workshop on Geometric Functionals: Analysis and Applications

proof_stages.label: Coarse volume-entropy framework
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up locally compact metric spaces with proper cocompact discrete isometry actions and invariant Borel measure, and defines entropy as the exponential growth rate of volumes of balls.

proof_stages.label: Group and manifold incarnations
proof_stages.summary: The general entropy specializes to Cayley graphs of finitely generated groups and to universal covers of closed Riemannian manifolds, leading to the classical Milnor–Schwarz comparison that detects only positivity of growth.

proof_stages.label: Curvature-free motivation
proof_stages.summary: Ricci comparison with hyperbolic space suggests treating entropy and Gromov hyperbolicity as coarse substitutes for lower and upper curvature control in settings where tensorial curvature is unavailable.

proof_stages.label: Choice-free entropy invariants and positivity tests
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines algebraic entropy as the infimum over finite generating sets and minimal entropy as the volume-normalized infimum over Riemannian metrics, then reviews known computable or positive cases for manifolds and groups.

proof_stages.label: Failure of unrestricted comparison
proof_stages.summary: Collapsing examples show that, without extra hypotheses, positive algebraic entropy of the fundamental group need not force positive minimal entropy of the manifold.

proof_stages.label: Main lower-bound theorem
proof_stages.summary: For an orientable essential closed n-manifold whose fundamental group is torsion-free, nonelementary, δ-hyperbolic, and has bounded Cayley entropy, the minimal entropy is bounded below by a positive constant depending on n, δ, and the entropy bound.

proof_stages.label: Margulis constant and filling-radius bridge
proof_stages.summary: The proof introduces the Margulis constant via cyclicity of short-loop subgroups and the filling radius via the Kuratowski embedding, then uses Gromov–Sabourau inequalities to obtain a lower volume bound from the Margulis constant.

proof_stages.label: Quantitative Tits alternative and free semigroups
proof_stages.summary: A uniform Tits-type alternative for torsion-free nonelementary δ-hyperbolic groups bounds the algebraic entropy of every finitely generated noncyclic subgroup from below, using free semigroups to produce growth, and this group-theoretic ingredient is combined with the filling-radius bridge to prove the theorem.

examples.name: Hyperbolic manifold times RP^3
